behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate: test only the payload-builder function with static fixtures; catches field-mapping bugs inside one function, but misses real handoff failures where source adapters return differently shaped data and one module drops a source. Integration candidate: wire source clients + orchestration + summary-step boundary (summary mocked only as a sink) and assert the payload includes metrics, issues, and deployment sections. This behavior’s failure is at module boundaries (collector/orchestrator/handoff), so MOCK-MISS says Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required because source calls can be in-process stubs. LLM-DEP: behavior checks inclusion before generation quality, so no real model judgment required. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is about "content passed to the summary-generation step," not real network behavior or summary quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with three stubbed source adapters returning distinct sentinel values; inject a fake summary generator that records its input; assert recorded input contains data from all three sources.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate: test exception-handling helper returns fallback text; catches local fallback logic, but misses whether orchestrator continues other sources and still emits a full report sectioned correctly. Integration candidate: orchestrator + section formatter + source adapters (one adapter fails) and assert report completion plus explicit "data unavailable" note in the failed section. MOCK-MISS: this specific failure appears when modules interact after one source failure. REAL-INFRA: can reproduce with an in-process failing stub; no real network outage machinery needed. LLM-DEP: correctness here is fallback behavior, not model output quality. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior does not require proving real network stack behavior; rejected Agentic because behavior text requires a deterministic fallback note, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute report generation with one source adapter raising timeout/error and two adapters returning valid data; verify pipeline does not abort and final report contains a `data unavailable` marker for exactly that source section.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: stub summary generator with fixed text and assert downstream formatting; catches plumbing only, but always passes even if real model omits/misstates key values. Agentic candidate: call the real summary model on representative inputs and score factual alignment (key metrics/issues/deploy counts) with acceptance thresholds over repeated runs. MOCK-HIDE and deterministic-assertion trap apply: a mock replaces model judgment, hiding the real failure. MULTI-STEP: single model decision, so Agentic not Workflow. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on one summary-generation outcome, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed evaluation dataset with known key values; invoke the real summary-generation model; evaluate whether outputs preserve required facts (e.g., numeric values, top issues, deployment status) across multiple trials with pass-rate threshold.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Integration candidate: mock email sender and assert `send()` called; catches orchestration call path, but misses real SMTP/API auth, transport, MIME rendering, and inbox acceptance failures. System candidate: run full script against real test endpoints/mail sandbox and verify message appears in recipient inbox with expected report artifact. REAL-INFRA: explicit inbox delivery requires real external email behavior. LLM-DEP: this behavior is delivery correctness, not evaluating model quality; summary step can be fixed/stubbed if needed. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior text is "reaches ... inbox correctly," which disqualifies model-quality dependency as the primary failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: In a staging environment, execute the scheduled script end-to-end using real email service credentials and a test recipient inbox; assert message receipt, subject/body presence, and attachment/content delivery integrity.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Unit candidate: test retry-state function writes a flag/path; catches local state logic, but misses cross-module behavior of (a) handling send failure, (b) persisting report, (c) reloading and retrying on next run. Integration candidate: run pipeline twice in-process with real local filesystem (temp dir) and failing-then-recovered email adapter to verify persistence and retry. REAL-INFRA: no real external network is required; failure can be simulated at adapter boundary. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: local filesystem is allowed at Integration; no mandatory real subprocess/OS primitive beyond standard file I/O; no LLM dependency; no multi-agentic chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text requires "saved to a local file" and "retried on the next scheduled run," both testable by two controlled invocations without real scheduler infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: First run: email adapter returns unreachable error; assert report file is written to retry store. Second run: adapter succeeds; assert stored report is retried/sent and retry file/state is cleared.
